name: Funding — Washington Local Programs (WTIA, Commerce, WWU iLab, Whatcom SBDC)
outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/wa_local_funding.md exists with: (a) ≥6 WA-state or Bellingham-local programs covered, (b) per program — type (grant/advisor/credits/space/network), eligibility, application or intro process, contact, typical award size, (c) which can be approached this month with a 30-min intro meeting, (d) talking points for initial outreach.
anti_requirements: Do NOT include national programs in this report — WA / Pacific Northwest / Bellingham only.
Do NOT invent contacts — every named individual must trace to a public source URL.
Do NOT recommend approaching programs that explicitly serve only post-revenue or post-Series-A startups.
Do NOT contact any program — research and prep talking points only.

jobs.label: wtia_research
jobs.description: Research Washington Technology Industry Association (WTIA) programs
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/wtia.md catalogs WTIA programs relevant to a solo pre-revenue AI infra founder.
jobs.prompt:
Research Washington Technology Industry Association (washingtontechnology.org or equivalent). Find:
- Member tier options (which is appropriate for a solo pre-revenue founder)
- Active grant or program offerings (e.g. Cascadia Innovation Corridor, founder programs)
- Mentorship / advisor programs
- Events / community access
- Cost (membership fees if any)
- Application or intro contact path

Write `artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/wtia.md` with one section per relevant offering. For each: name, type, eligibility, cost, contact, source URL.

jobs.label: wa_commerce_research
jobs.description: Research WA Department of Commerce / StartUp Washington programs
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/wa_commerce.md catalogs WA Department of Commerce and StartUp Washington offerings for solo founders.
jobs.prompt:
Research Washington Department of Commerce (commerce.wa.gov) and StartUp Washington (startup.choosewashingtonstate.com or equivalent). Find:
- Active grant programs for early-stage tech businesses
- Tax credit programs (R&D, small business, etc.)
- Resource directories (linking to other programs)
- Direct contact for solo founder questions

Write `artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/wa_commerce.md` with one section per offering. For each: name, type, eligibility, application URL, contact, source URL.

jobs.label: wwu_ilab_research
jobs.description: Research Western Washington University iLab / entrepreneurship programs (Bellingham)
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/wwu_ilab.md catalogs WWU's entrepreneurship resources accessible to a Bellingham community founder.
jobs.prompt:
Research Western Washington University's entrepreneurship and innovation resources — search for WWU iLab, WWU Center for Innovation, WWU SBDC affiliation, or equivalent. Note: many of these programs serve both students and the surrounding community.

Find:
- Whether community founders (non-students) can access programs
- Available programs: incubator/accelerator, mentorship, micro-grants, lab/space access
- Cost
- Intro contact path

Write `artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/wwu_ilab.md`. If non-students cannot access, say so explicitly.

jobs.label: whatcom_sbdc_research
jobs.description: Research Washington Small Business Development Center (Whatcom County)
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/whatcom_sbdc.md catalogs Whatcom County SBDC services for a solo tech founder.
jobs.prompt:
Research the Washington Small Business Development Center (wsbdc.org) Whatcom County office. Find:
- Free advisor services (1:1 business counseling)
- Workshops and training
- Microloan or grant referrals they facilitate
- Local advisor name and contact (Bellingham-based if listed)
- How to schedule an intro meeting

Write `artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/whatcom_sbdc.md`. SBDC is free — confirm and document any caveats.

jobs.label: bellingham_econ_dev_research
jobs.description: Research City of Bellingham / Whatcom County / Port of Bellingham economic development programs
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/bellingham_econ_dev.md catalogs local economic development programs accessible to a tech founder.
jobs.prompt:
Research economic development programs from:
- City of Bellingham (cob.org)
- Port of Bellingham
- Whatcom County government economic development
- Bellingham Regional Chamber of Commerce

Look for: small business grants, loan programs, tech-sector support, networking events, broadband/infrastructure assistance for home-based businesses.

Write `artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/bellingham_econ_dev.md`. Most local programs target brick-and-mortar — be honest about which are not applicable to a software founder.

jobs.label: innovate_washington_research
jobs.description: Research Innovate Washington and other state-level innovation programs
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/innovate_washington.md catalogs Innovate Washington and adjacent state-level innovation programs.
jobs.prompt:
Research Innovate Washington (or its current successor entity if rebranded), Life Sciences Washington (in case AI-for-bio is in scope), and any other state-level innovation programs that fund or support early-stage tech founders.

Write `artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/innovate_washington.md` with one section per program. For each: status (active/dormant/sunset), eligibility, type of support, contact.

jobs.label: compose_contact_list
jobs.description: Produce the final operator-facing contact list with talking points
jobs.outcome_goal: artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/wa_local_funding.md is the operator-facing deliverable with ranked outreach order, contacts, and talking points per program.
jobs.prompt:
Read all six program files under `artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/programs/`.

Produce `artifacts/funding/wa_local_20260501/wa_local_funding.md` with:

1. **TL;DR** (3 sentences: which 2–3 programs to approach this month and why)
2. **Outreach order** (numbered list of programs ranked by expected return-on-30-min — programs that offer free advisor time first, paid memberships last)
3. **Per-program section**:
   - Heading: `## <Program name>`
   - Quick facts: type, eligibility, cost, contact (name + phone/email), schedule-link if available
   - **Why approach now / why later / why never** — one line
   - **Talking points for the 30-min intro** — 3 bullets framing what Nate wants to learn (not what he wants them to do for him)
4. **Disqualified programs** — short list of programs from the inputs that are not applicable, with one-line reason each

Framing for the talking points: Nate is a solo founder in Bellingham building Praxis (autonomous engineering control plane), pre-revenue, target external launch 2026-07-19. He's looking for: (a) connection to other WA founders, (b) signal on which state/local programs are worth applying to, (c) advisor time on go-to-market.
